$schema: Each evidence record describes a single source document. 'proves' lists what the record establishes; 'doesNotProve' is what it CANNOT establish (so future claims don't overstate).

ev-1775-nc-deed-boston-best.extract: Names Boston Best with wife Catron Best.
ev-1775-nc-deed-boston-best.citation: 1775 North Carolina deed (Boston Best Sr. and wife Catron).
proves: Boston Best Sr. existed and was married
Wife's recorded name was Catron
doesNotProve: Catron's full given name (likely Catherine variant — not certain)

ev-1779-marriage-peter-cristina.extract: Peter Best m. Cristina Whitenbarger; bondsman: Boston Best.
proves: Peter Best and Cristina Whitenbarger married 13 Oct 1779
Boston Best stood as bondsman (close associate, often kin)
doesNotProve: Peter is Boston's son (only that they were close enough for Boston to bond)

ev-1809-boston-best-estate.extract: Estate of Boston Best Sr. administered.
proves: Boston Best Sr. died by 1809
Estate was administered in Lincoln County, NC
doesNotProve: Heirs (no named list survives in this record)

ev-1809-boston-estate-peter-account.extract: 'Estate of Boston Best Senr to Peter Best Dr … Peter Best made oath…'
proves: Peter Best had direct financial involvement in Boston Best Sr.'s estate consistent with immediate family/heir
doesNotProve: Peter is explicitly named as son or heir
Exact relationship — could be creditor, agent, or kin

ev-1809-eve-bess-petition.extract: Petition involving Eve Bess; sister of Elizabeth Line; children include John Bess and Polly Bess.
proves: Eve Bess existed in Lincoln Co. NC 1809
Sister to Elizabeth Line
Children John and Polly Bess

ev-1832-peter-bess-will.extract: Will of Peter Bess; wife Leannah; estate to be divided 'among my children' (children unnamed).
ev-1832-peter-bess-will.citation: 1832 Peter Bess will, Lincoln County, NC.
proves: Peter Bess existed in Lincoln Co. NC, died ~1832
Wife Leannah
Had children (unnamed)
doesNotProve: Whether this is the same Peter Best who married Cristina Whitenbarger 1779

ev-1822-tax-list-jacob-bess.citation: 1822 Cape Girardeau County, MO tax list.
proves: Jacob Bess present in Cape Girardeau Co. MO by 1822

ev-1830-census-jacob-bess.extract: Male age 30–39 (born ~1790–1800); household with multiple children.
ev-1830-census-jacob-bess.citation: 1830 U.S. Census, Cape Girardeau Co., MO.
proves: Jacob Bess head of household 1830
Birth range ~1790–1800
Had multiple children in household

ev-1850-census-jacob-bess.extract: Jacob Bess, age 52, born North Carolina; household includes Matilda, Emanuel, Barbary, Jacob, Edward, Daniel.
ev-1850-census-jacob-bess.citation: 1850 U.S. Census, Cape Girardeau Co., MO.
proves: Jacob Bess age ~52 in 1850 (born ~1798)
Reported NC birthplace
Household members named
doesNotProve: Exact relationships within household (1850 census did not record relationship to head)
Wife's identity (no adult woman matching wife age in this household)

ev-jacob-bess-probate.extract: Jacob Bess deceased; Matilda Bess administratrix.
proves: Jacob Bess died before Feb 1864 in Bollinger Co. MO
Matilda Bess served as administratrix
doesNotProve: Matilda's exact relationship to Jacob (administratrix is often a relative but not always)

ev-jacob-bess-partition-1868.extract: Heirs listed: Sarah (m. James Bess), Catherine, Adaline (m. Rhodes), Barbara/Barbary (m. Blaylock), Mary A., Jacob Jr., Edward, Daniel, Emanuel, Levi, James.
ev-jacob-bess-partition-1868.citation: Jacob Bess Sr. partition/distribution case, Bollinger Co., MO ~1868.
proves: These individuals are Jacob Bess Sr.'s legal heirs
Married names of daughters (Sarah-James Bess, Adaline-Rhodes, Barbara-Blaylock)
doesNotProve: Whether these are all biological children (heirs can include grandchildren by representation)

ev-1859-jacob-bess-deed.extract: Jacob Bess appears alone (no wife joining in dower).
proves: By 1859 Jacob Bess transacted property without a wife joining
doesNotProve: That he had never had a wife (dower customs and missing women in deeds vary)

ev-1857-edward-bess-deed.extract: Edward B. Bess and Jane Bess his wife; Jane relinquishes dower.
proves: Edward B. Bess married to a Jane by 1857
Continuing marriage of Edward B. Bess and Jane in 1866
Edward and Jane resided in Madison Co. MO by 1872

ev-1857-levi-bess-deed.extract: Levi Bess and Barbary Bess his wife; dower released.
proves: Levi Bess married to a Barbary by 1857
doesNotProve: That this Barbary is the same as any other Barbara/Barbary in the file

ev-1880-census-roddi-bess.extract: 'Roddi Bess,' daughter of Edward and Jane.
proves: Roddi Bess is daughter of Edward and Jane Bess in 1880
doesNotProve: Roddi = Rhoda (treated as same person by later records — supported but technically a name match)

ev-albert-ransom-bess-death.extract: Albert Ransom Bess; born 11 May 1862, Bessville, MO; parents: Edward Bess and Jane Sanders.
ev-albert-ransom-bess-death.citation: 1935 Illinois death record for Albert Ransom Bess.
proves: Birth/death dates and places of Albert Ransom Bess
Strongest source for Jane's maiden name = Sanders
doesNotProve: Maiden name with absolute certainty (death-record informant could be wrong; spelling Sanders/Saunders varies)

ev-1847-hiram-bess-will.extract: Hiram Bess; wife Eliza; children John Franklin, Catherine, Margaret Elizabeth, Susan Adeline, Mary Ann.
proves: Hiram Bess's wife Eliza and named children

ev-1855-boston-bess-guardian-bond.extract: Boston Bess guardian bond for Hiram's minor children.
proves: Boston Bess (1869) acted as guardian for Hiram Bess's minor children in 1855

ev-1869-boston-bess-letters.extract: Letters testamentary; widow Elizabeth Bess; executor Thomas Bess.
ev-1869-boston-bess-letters.citation: 1869 Lincoln Co., NC letters testamentary, Boston Bess.
proves: Boston Bess (1869) died with widow Elizabeth and executor Thomas Bess
doesNotProve: This is NOT the same Boston as Boston Best Sr. (d. 1809)

ev-1869-boston-estate-cluster.extract: 1869 Boston Bess estate event includes Jacob, Lawson, and Peter Bess together.
proves: Jacob, Lawson, and Peter Bess associated in Missouri ~1869

ev-frederick-slinker-nc-patents.extract: NC land patents to Frederick Slinker.
proves: Frederick Slinker held land in NC by 1768
Frederick Slinker continued landholding

ev-frederick-slinker-1794-deed.location: Lincoln County, North Carolina (Flat Rock / Catawba region)
proves: Frederick Slinker present in NC 1794

ev-1788-jacob-slinkard-heir-deed.extract: '…descended… to Jacob… heir at law' (referring to land of Frederick Slinker).
proves: A Jacob Slinkard was 'heir at law' of Frederick Slinker per inheritance language
doesNotProve: That Jacob is explicitly Frederick's son (heir-at-law can be other relations)
That this Jacob is the same as MO Jacob Slinkard

proves: Jacob Slinkard owned and sold land in Cape Girardeau Co. MO 1818

ev-1823-jacob-slinkard-deceased-deed.extract: '…land confirmed to Jacob Slinkard deceased and owned by him in his life time…' Includes Barbary Best wife of Jacob Best as party.
proves: Jacob Slinkard (MO) died before 1 Oct 1823
Barbara/Barbary identified as wife of Jacob Best
Land cluster involves John Best & Catherine, Daniel Slinkard & wife Hannah, David Kinder

ev-jacob-miller-guardianship-barbara.extract: 'Jacob Miller, guardian of Barbara Slinkard… paid her share to her husband Jacob Best…'
ev-jacob-miller-guardianship-barbara.citation: Probate / guardian distribution, Cape Girardeau Co., MO.
proves: Barbara Slinkard had a guardian (her father deceased)
Barbara Slinkard married Jacob Best
Identification of Jacob Best as Barbara's husband
doesNotProve: Identity of Barbara's father by name in this record alone (must combine with 1823 deed)
Polly Slinkard's guardian in this record (see ev-circuit-court-cape-jacob-miller-guardian for both sisters together)

ev-circuit-court-cape-jacob-miller-guard.extract: 'Jacob Miller, Guardian of Polly Slinkerd, daughter of Jacob Slinkard deceased…' 'Jacob Miller, Guardian of Barbara Slinkerd, daughter of Jacob Slinkard deceased…'
ev-circuit-court-cape-jacob-miller-guard.citation: Circuit Court Proceedings, Cape Girardeau County, MO (early court term, likely 1830s).
proves: Polly Slinkard explicitly named as 'daughter of Jacob Slinkard deceased' in a court record
Barbara Slinkard explicitly named as 'daughter of Jacob Slinkard deceased' in a court record
Jacob Miller was guardian of both Polly and Barbara simultaneously
Both were minors at the same time (shared guardian, same proceeding)
Polly and Barbara Slinkard are siblings
doesNotProve: That this is the same proceeding as ev-jacob-miller-guardianship-barbara (that record is a probate distribution; this is a circuit court term — may be separate)

ev-1820-best-slinkard-deed.extract: John Best & Catharine convey land to Daniel Slinkard.
proves: John Best transacted land with Daniel Slinkard 1820
doesNotProve: Family relationship between Best and Slinkard

ev-1838-daniel-slinkard-probate.extract: Estate of Daniel Slinkard opened; widow Eve Slinkard; heirs: John, Jacob, Catharine, Polly, David, Emanuel, Denney (Denny), Ramon (Ransom), Nancy, Nora, Daniel (Jr.), Rosey.
ev-1838-daniel-slinkard-probate.citation: 1838 Daniel Slinkard probate, Cape Girardeau Co., MO.
proves: Daniel Slinkard died before Nov 1838
Widow Eve
Named heirs
Marriage index entry exists for Daniel Slinkard and Eve Morrison
doesNotProve: That this Daniel is the same Daniel who d. 1838 (probable but not certain)

proves: A Frederick Slinker held land in MO 1818 adjacent to Mathias Bollinger
doesNotProve: This is the same Frederick Slinker who held NC land

proves: Frederick Slinker active in MO court matters 1833
Marriage of William Best and Louise Best 1818
doesNotProve: Their relationship to John or Jacob Best

proves: Joseph Best married Hannah 1829
Henry Slinkard married Rebecca 1814 in NC

ev-1816-peter-critz-guardian-settlement.extract: Peter Critz guardian of David & Jacob Slinkard (minors); settlement.
proves: Two minor Slinkards (David, Jacob) had Peter Critz as guardian as of 1816
ev-1816-peter-critz-guardian-settlement.notes: May be the same proceeding as ev-jacob-slinkard-mo-minors-guardianship — pending user verification.

ev-jacob-slinkard-mo-minors-guardianship.extract: Guardianship/court record naming Jacob Slinkard (deceased) and his minor children: Polly Slinkard, Barbara Slinkard, David Slinkard, Jacob Slinkard.
ev-jacob-slinkard-mo-minors-guardianship.citation: User-provided summary; original record citation TBD.
proves: Polly, Barbara, David, and Jacob Slinkard are all children of a Jacob Slinkard who was deceased at the time of the proceeding
doesNotProve: Whether this 'deceased Jacob Slinkard' is the same individual as the Jacob Slinkard who sold a town lot in 1818 — see conflict note
Maiden name of mother (mother not named here)
ev-jacob-slinkard-mo-minors-guardianship.notes: TEMPORAL CONFLICT WITH ev-1818-jacob-slinkard-mo-deed. The 1818 deed has Jacob alive. If the date range ~1813–1816 is correct AND this is the same Jacob, the 1818 deed cannot also refer to him. Possibilities: (a) date range is actually later (e.g. ~1819–1822); (b) two different Jacob Slinkards in MO; (c) 1818 deed refers to a different Jacob. See review/conflicts.md and task t-jacob-slinkard-guardianship-date.

ev-1827-elizabeth-gross-marriage.extract: Elizabeth Slinkard m. William Gross (also Grass).
proves: Elizabeth Slinkard married William Gross 20 May 1827
A Catharine Slinkard married Ruben Smith 29 Jan 1828
doesNotProve: Whether same as Catharine in Daniel's 1838 probate

proves: A Slinkard recorded as 'Olly' married Zachariah Crump 27 Apr 1830
A Katherine Slinkard married Aaron Link 1834
doesNotProve: Whether same as Catharine of 1828 marriage or 1838 probate

proves: Joseph Slinkerd married Alley Shrum 11 Dec 1836
doesNotProve: Whether same as other Josephs in file

proves: Jacob Slinkard married Phebe Hubble 1 Apr 1837
doesNotProve: His parents directly (though identification as Daniel's son is supported by 1838 probate)

proves: A John Slinkard married Viney Stroder 8 Dec 1839
doesNotProve: Whether same as John in marriage index entry naming Nancy Stroder

proves: Marriage index entry exists for Joseph Slinkard and Anna Green
doesNotProve: Whether same as Joseph–Alley Shrum 1836 or Joseph–Tennessee Estes

proves: Marriage index entry exists for John Slinkard and Nancy Stroder
doesNotProve: Whether same as John–Viney Stroder 1839 (Nancy/Viney could be transcription variants — or two different Johns)

ev-1850-jacob-slinkard-1812-census.extract: Jacob Slinkard household; Phebe; children William J, Daniel M (age 11), Ebenezer, Eliza C, Araminta C.
ev-1850-jacob-slinkard-1812-census.citation: 1850 U.S. Census, Cape Girardeau Co., MO.
proves: Jacob Slinkard (~1812) and Phebe with named children in 1850

ev-1860-jacob-slinkard-1812-census.location: Byrd Township, Cape Girardeau County, Missouri
ev-1860-jacob-slinkard-1812-census.extract: Same Jacob/Phebe family; adds John M, George W, Jacob L (infant); Daniel M now age 19.
ev-1860-jacob-slinkard-1812-census.citation: 1860 U.S. Census, Byrd Twp., Cape Girardeau Co., MO.
proves: Continuity of Jacob (~1812)/Phebe family 1850→1860 with additional children

ev-1867-daniel-m-estate-inventory.extract: Estate inventory of Daniel M. Slinkard.
proves: Daniel M. Slinkard died by 1867

ev-1876-mo-state-census-slinkard-cluster.extract: Phoebe Slinkard, Araminta Slinkard, George W Slinkard, John M Slinkard, Jacob L Slinkard, Robert L Slinkard, Marion B Slinkard.
proves: These Slinkards present in same township 1876

ev-1881-bollinger-tax-book-slinkards.extract: Slinkards listed: Sarah, J.F., M.B., Elizabeth, B.H., Joseph W., P.G., Daniel, Peter, Wm R., J.M., John F., James C.
proves: These individuals owned taxable property in Bollinger Co. 1881
Joseph Slinkard received federal land patent 1852 in Bollinger Co.

ev-joseph-slinkard-bollinger-probate.extract: Estate of Joseph Slinkard (deceased); real estate ordered sold; creditor: N. W. Slinkard.
proves: Joseph Slinkard (Bollinger) died with estate sold
N. W. Slinkard was a creditor

ev-ransom-estes-will.extract: '…daughter Tennessee Slinkard… married to Joseph Slinkard.'
proves: Tennessee Estes was Ransom B. Estes's daughter and married Joseph Slinkard
Elizabeth Bess married Israel Spencer per index
doesNotProve: Her family / connection to direct line

ev-1850-jacob-bess-cluster-analysis.extract: Jacob Bess Sr. fits chronologically (~1798) as second-generation son of Peter Best (b. ~1765–1775); migration timing aligns with NC→MO ~1815–1822; no conflicting candidate.
ev-1850-jacob-bess-cluster-analysis.citation: Working analysis combining the above sources.
doesNotProve: Direct parent-child relationship between Peter Best and Jacob Bess Sr.
ev-1850-jacob-bess-cluster-analysis.notes: Analytical record only; supports SUPPORTED status, never PROVEN.
